column2: A {{c1::time-based one-time password (TOTP)}} derives a short-lived code from a shared secret and the current time.
column3: TOTP is commonly generated by an authenticator application. Its time basis distinguishes it from one-time-code methods based on a different changing value.
column4: TOTP is a type of OTP. The important extra property is that time causes the code to expire rather than the code remaining valid indefinitely.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 26

column2: An authentication code intended to be valid for only one use is a {{c1::one-time password or passcode (OTP)}}.
column3: Hardware and software tokens can generate OTPs. TOTP is a more specific form whose changing value is tied to time.
column4: “One-time” describes reuse, while “time-based” describes how a TOTP changes and expires. Keep the category and its time-based subtype distinct.
